issues: The drafted headline treats a sentence from the article body as the headline. The OCR does not show a distinct standalone headline — the bolded/large-text headline is not clearly separated from the body text in the OCR. The sentence 'Rustlers blew a chance to put the Richmond Sockeyes in cold storage' appears to be the opening of the article body, not a formal headline.

The drafted short description incorrectly states 'series tied 3-3' without noting this was Game 6 of the series (i.e., after falling 7-3 the series is tied 3-3, which is accurate, but the game number context is missing).

The long description attributes 'notes the Rustlers' unpredictable season throughout' as a separate observation by the reporter, when in fact it is the article's own lede framing — a minor but misleading paraphrase.
The byline should be 'Greg Meachem, The Advocate' (the OCR reads 'By GREG MEACHEM of The Advocate'), not simply 'Greg Meachem'.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: The Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Red Deer Rustlers 7-3 in Game 6 of the Western Centennial Cup semifinal, tying the best-of-seven series at 3-3 with the deciding game that evening.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Red Deer Rustlers 7-3 on April 15, 1987, before 2,020 fans at Red Deer Arena, tying the best-of-seven Western Centennial Cup semifinal series at 3-3. The deciding Game 7 was scheduled for that evening at 7:30 p.m. at the Arena. Reporter Greg Meachem of The Advocate opens by characterizing the Rustlers' season as totally unpredictable — losing games they were expected to win and winning at unexpected times — framing their Game 6 collapse as consistent with that pattern.
review.notes: The OCR does not show a clear standalone headline separate from the article body. The phrase 'Rustlers blew a chance to put the Richmond Sockeyes in cold storage' reads as the article's lede sentence rather than a typeset headline — editor should confirm from the scan image whether a formal headline exists above the byline. Also note: the game described took place on Wednesday (April 15), published April 16; the short draft's date reference is technically the publication date, which is fine, but the game itself was the prior evening.
